question: Is the edit applied specifically to the foremost Tibetan woman (closest to the camera)?
Is the foremost woman's inner robe (sleeves and top) colored sapphire blue?
Is the robe completely free of the original red color?
Has the foremost woman's waist apron (bangdian) been replaced with a striped pattern?
Does the new apron pattern include visible purple stripes?
Does the new apron pattern include visible green stripes?
Does the new apron pattern include visible red stripes?
Is the foremost woman's black outer vest/chuba preserved in its original black color?
Are the foremost woman's face and braided hairstyle unchanged?
Is the prayer wheel held by the foremost woman unchanged in shape and appearance?
Are the clothing colors of the other women in the scene unchanged?
Does the newly colored robe maintain realistic fabric folds and shading?
Is the edited image free from color bleeding (e.g., blue spilling onto the black vest or background)?
Is the lighting on the edited areas consistent with the global scene lighting?
